| **Phil Scott’s ‘The High Road’ Video the Most Viewed of any this Primary Race; Scott the Most Endorsed Candidate**
_Tuesday, August 9 th Vermonters can send a message about the type of candidates and campaigns they want in Vermont_
Montpelier, Vt. – The Phil Scott for Vermont campaign today announced their online video, "The High Road," is the most watched video of the 2016 Vermont gubernatorial primary election.

Scott has also earned, by far, the most Republican endorsements because of his opposition to new tax increases, plans for reducing state spending and unwavering commitment to reversing Vermont’s crisis of affordability.

"The High Road” video premiered just last week and features Scott rising above the negative, unprecedented campaign tactics of his opponent and instead focusing on his two key priorities: Growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable.

“In the field, at events, and online, the response from Vermonters has been overwhelmingly positive. From the team’s standpoint, it is very reassuring to see Vermonters so receptive to Phil’s positive, issues-based campaign when our opponent is being incredibly dishonest," said Brittney Wilson, spokeswoman for the Scott campaign. “Phil tells us every day that ‘If we are serious about changing the tone and the quality of elections here and across the country, we have to be willing to lead by example.’ In the face of incredibly dishonest smear tactics and attack ads, we are proud to stand up for everyone who wants an honest discussion of the issues and how we can all work together to reform state government, strengthen the economy and make Vermont affordable for families and businesses.”

**"Vermonters have the opportunity to make a real statement on Tuesday about the kind of political campaigns they want to see in Vermont by voting for Phil Scott**," Wilson added. “At Phil’s direction we’re going to continue to take the high road, all the way to victory in November, and we hope Vermonters of all political persuasions will turn out on August 9th and vote in the Republican primary to send a crystal clear message about the type of campaigns and candidates they want in Vermont.”

View the Scott Campaign’s most watched video here: | [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cej6pz9HTbo)
---

Heading into Tuesday’s Primary, Scott has also earned the most endorsements of any candidate, with supporters acknowledging Scott's economic priorities, focus on the issues impacting Vermonters, and positive approach.

**Roger Marcoux, Sheriff of Lamoille County** , for example, said the Vermont Sheriffs’ Association endorsed Phil Scott for Governor because of “his [leadership] and commitment to combatting opiate addiction and its collateral damage in this state.” Brian Hoar, on behalf of the Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association (VADA), also endorsed Phil for his leadership saying, “He is the only candidate who has focused all of his attention on growing the economy and making Vermont more affordable; and he is the only candidate with the leadership ability to build the bipartisan coalition necessary to get real results.”

In addition, **Phil Scott has the support of more than 100 Republican legislators, former legislators, candidates, and party officials**. Highlighting this very long list of endorsements are **Jim Douglas** , former Governor of Vermont, and **Randy Brock** , former Franklin County Senator and State Auditor. Last week, in a press conference in Colchester, Randy Brock said he decided to endorse Phil Scott because “[Phil] is a fighter for working Vermonters and will oppose all new taxes. Most importantly, he is a leader with tremendous integrity who we can count on to restore faith and trust in the Governor’s office.”

**Listen, Learn, Lead: Phil Scott**
Phil Scott is a consistent voice of reason and common sense in Montpelier. As the only Republican elected to statewide office, Phil shows determination and leadership in advocating for Vermonters and promoting policies and programs that benefit Vermont families and the economy. That’s why he has the support of more than 100 current and former Republican leaders, including Governor Jim Douglas and former State Auditor Randy Brock. Read about Phil Scott’s plans at _[www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c350d0c9e8&e=19cebfe173)_.

Contact: Ethan Latour
[Ethan@philscott.org](mailto:ethan@philscott.org)
_802-595-3776_
**Phil Scott Finishes Primary Campaign with** **“High Road to Victory” Tour**
Middlesex, Vt. – With just one day to go before Vermonters nominate their candidates for Governor, Phil Scott’s high-energy campaign is accelerating to victory and doubling down on the candidate’s positive, issues-driven approach with the “High Road to Victory” tour.

The tour, which comes just days after his opponent leveled another series of dishonest accusations, will highlight Scott’s continued commitment to running a campaign focused on his priorities of growing the economy and making Vermont affordable for families and businesses. Last week Scott also released a new ad entitled “[The High Road](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7814943186&e=19cebfe173)” where he says his team will “rise above” the negative attacks and set an example.

Scott said, “If we are serious about changing the tone and the quality of elections and politics – and I am very serious about that – we have to be willing to lead by example. My opponent has spent nearly $2 million misleading Vermonters about my plans, my priorities - and in the last week, my integrity. **We’re going to rise above it.** I’ve never been more resolved to stand up for the Vermonters who want an honest discussion about how we can work together to reform state government, strengthen the economy and make Vermont affordable for every family.”

The choice in this election is clear, Scott continued. "Vermonters have an opportunity to make a statement on Tuesday about the kind of political campaigns – and the type of candidates – they want to see in Vermont," he said. "My team and I are going to continue to take the high road, and I believe the high road will lead us to victory on Tuesday and again in November. With your help and your vote we will do it the Vermont way.”

**__Let’s Rise Above It__**** _Tour Schedule_**
* **8:30 AM – 9:45 AM: St. Albans, Exit 19 Intersection \-- Open Press**

* **10:00 AM – 10:30 AM: South Burlington, Shelburne Road – Open Press**(Near the Bagel Bakery, 992 Shelburne Rd.)

* **12:00 PM – 1:00 PM: Rutland, Intersection of Route 4 & Route 7 – Open Press**

* **3:00 – 4:00PM: Barre, City Hall Park – Open Press**

* **5:00 – 6:00PM: Morrisville, Intersection of Routes 100 & 15 – Open Press **(Note: This is a Team Scott event; the candidate is at another event at this time.)

* **5:00 – 7:00PM Boyden Farm Meet & Greet/Fundraiser – RSVP/Press Credentials Required **(Note: Credentials required for media. Please call campaign HQ for more info.)

**TAKING THE HIGH ROAD TO VICTORY**

The tour, which comes just days after his opponent leveled another series of dishonest accusations, will highlight Scott’s continued commitment to running a campaign focused on his priorities of growing the economy and making Vermont affordable for families and businesses. Last week Scott also released a new ad entitled “” where he says his team will “rise above” the negative attacks and set an example. Scott said, “If we are serious about changing the tone and the quality of elections and politics – and I am very serious about that – we have to be willing to lead by example. My opponent has spent nearly $2 million misleading Vermonters about my plans, my priorities - and in the last week, my integrity. We’re going to rise above it. I’ve never been more resolved to stand up for the Vermonters who want an honest discussion about how we can work together to reform state government, strengthen the economy and make Vermont affordable for every family.” The choice in this election is clear, Scott continued. "Vermonters have an opportunity to make a statement on Tuesday about the kind of political campaigns – and the type of candidates – they want to see in Vermont," he said. "My team and I are going to continue to take the high road, and I believe the high road will lead us to victory on Tuesday and again in November. With your help and your vote we will do it the Vermont way.” Let’s Rise Above It Tour Schedule 8:30 AM – 9:45 AM: St. Albans, Exit 19 Intersection \-- Open Press 10:00 AM – 10:30 AM: South Burlington, Shelburne Road – Open Press(Near the Bagel Bakery, 992 Shelburne Rd.) 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM: Rutland, Intersection of Route 4 & Route 7 – Open Press 3:00 – 4:00PM: Barre, City Hall Park – Open Press 5:00 – 6:00PM: Morrisville, Intersection of Routes 100 & 15 – Open Press (Note: This is a Team Scott event; the candidate is at another event at this time.) 5:00 – 7:00PM Boyden Farm Meet & Greet/Fundraiser – RSVP/Press Credentials Required (Note: Credentials required for media. body:

That's a strong showing that raises the very real possibility that we could be outspent in this election. Getting outspent could mean getting out-advertised or even outworked on the ground.

name: TEAM COLE
subject: August Off to Busy Start
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:
| |
---
| Friend --
August is off to a busy start at the Cole for Governor campaign. Last Monday the campaign [announced](http://www.billcolewv.com/cole_endorsed_by_wv_auto_dealers?e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=1) that Bill received the backing of the West Virginia Automobile and Truck Dealers Association. It is the first time in history the organization has endorsed a candidate. They [join](http://www.billcolewv.com/cole_endorsements?e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=2) a growing group of job creators backing Bill Cole for Governor. Later in the week Bill [made it clear](http://www.billcolewv.com/justice_hides_from_voters_by_refusing_to_debate?e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=3) that he will participate in the two proposed media debates on October 4th and October 11th in Charleston. Meanwhile, _No Show Jim_ has refused to agree to participate in either of the debates and has indicated that he will only agree to one debate that includes ridiculous stipulations like the candidates need to be seated and the footage can’t be used. One leading political commentator with close contacts with the Justice campaign told a group at a private meeting that the Justice campaign doesn’t want him debating because they can’t control him. Former state Democratic Party Vice Chair Chris Regan weighed saying Jim Justice "absolutely" should debate Bill Cole. Bill also hit the campaign trail hard last week. He spent Thursday in Logan and Mingo counties where he stopped by the Logan Volunteer Fire Department. He also held meet and greets in [Logan](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBillColeWV%2Fstatus%2F760893600628486144&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=4) and [Mingo](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBillColeWV%2Fstatus%2F760987701814235136&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=5) counties that afternoon and evening. On Thursday, Bill [stood](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBillColeWV%2Fstatus%2F761490844222418944&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=6) before the local elected officials from across the state at the West Virginia Municipal League’s Annual Conference. Once again _No Show Jim_ Justice was nowhere to be seen. On Friday, he [stood ](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FBillColeWV%2Fstatus%2F761726986104352768&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=7)before the men and women of the West Virginia Coal Association and talked about his plan to create good paying jobs and revitalize the coal industry. The association extended their Primary Election endorsement to the General Election. Last night Bill was in charge of the grill at the Bill Cole Automall Annual Media Night event with the Bluefield Blue Jays and Princeton Rays with the proceeds going to fight childhood cancer and autism. As Bill [told](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.bdtonline.com%2Fnews%2Fvalue-of-local-baseball-clubs-recognized-with-annual-dinner%2Farticle_f84cfddc-5d18-11e6-afa3-cbfc3f26bb88.html&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=8) the _Bluefield Daily Telegraph_ , “Too many times we don’t know what we have until we lose it, and I hope our community will continue to understand of the value that the ball clubs bring. Bill Cole Automall and both ball clubs have joined in support of two great charitable foundations, the Jonathan Powell Hope Foundation and the Denver Foundation.” This week will be another busy week with Bill traveling the state and standing before voters to talk about his plans to create good paying jobs and tackle our state’s drug epidemic. Tonight he will hold a Town Hall meeting in Clarksburg on the Drug Epidemic. Tomorrow Bill will speak before the West Virginia Independent Oil and Gas Association before holding a tele-town hall with voters. On Thursday, Bill will be in Wheeling hosting another Town Hall on the need to create good paying jobs. The big news of the week was the start to the Olympics in Brazil where West Virginia University’s own Ginny Thrasher [won](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwvmetronews.com%2F2016%2F08%2F07%2Fafter-media-crush-ginny-thrasher-takes-aim-at-another-gold%2F&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=august_off&n=9) the first gold medal in the 10m air rifle competition. Congratulations to Ginny. She made West Virginia Proud. **TEAM COLE**

subject: New poll in governor's race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Justice for Governor
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/-5619624159773063168/-7277112710007486464?refcode=home)

**NEW POLL: Justice Holds 10 point lead over Cole**
Cole & RGA have already spent nearly $1 million on negative ads in general election

Today, the Jim Justice campaign released fresh polling numbers that show businessman Jim Justice is maintaining a commanding double-digit lead over Senate President Bill Cole in the governor's race.

Despite the early onslaught of television attack ads Bill Cole and the Republican Governors Association that started running immediately after the primary, Justice leads Cole in every general election poll. See polling memo below.

"Senator Cole's dishonest ads aren't fooling West Virginians about Jim Justice," said Grant Herring, a spokesman for the Justice campaign. "Jim Justice is leading in every poll because the people of West Virginia know he is the most prepared to grow jobs as governor. Bill Cole is a self-serving politician out to help himself and the voters aren't buying it."

To date, the Republican Governors Association has spent $550,000 on TV ads attacking Jim Justice, and Cole has spent $350,000 on his latest negative ad blitz. Plus, the RGA spent another $550,000 this spring propping up Bill Cole in his unopposed primary. Below is a memo from the pollster:

TO: Interested Parties
FROM: Global Strategy Group
DATE: August 8, 2016
RE: POLLING MEMO - WEST VIRGINIA 2016 GUBERNATORIAL RACE
__________________________________________________________

Jim Justice has 10 point lead in race for Governor

In a telephone survey of likely 2016 General Election voters, Jim Justice leads Republican Bill Cole, 47% to 37% in the race for Governor. Just 15% of voters are undecided. These results are based on a live telephone survey of 419 likely General Election voters conducted by Global Strategy Group from August 1-3, 2016.

Global Strategy Group 8/1-3, 419 interviews
Jim Justice -- 47%
Bill Cole -- 37%
Undecided -- 15%

ABOUT THIS POLL
The results of this statewide survey have a margin of error of +/-4.8%. Care has been taken to ensure the geographic and demographic divisions of the expected electorate are properly represented based on past voter turnout statistics.

Paid for by Justice for Governor. Public employees please disregard.

Have you RSVPed for Super Saturday?

My finance director just shared with me our projection report - and I'm in danger of falling behind. Here are the numbers:

name: Team Bullock
subject: stark differences
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Steve Bullock - Democrat, P
body:
---
Friend,

| [](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=%2FKlXoG35c1Eg%2B6%2BL7GlF18usVmiGtNq9)
---

Women make 74 cents on the dollar compared to men in Montana. It's not just unfair -- this disparity has a direct impact on the finances of families across the state. Women stand to lose hundreds of thousands of dollars over the course of a lifetime and that makes it all the more difficult to afford basic expenses, much less save for college or plan for retirement.

That's why in 2013, Steve put together an Equal Pay for Equal Work Task Force and four days ago signed an executive order championing their recommendations.

**[Help reelect Steve so he can continue this important work by pitching in $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=yKBs1UaEqpb%2BXtt%2FmZwzVsusVmiGtNq9)**

**Let's be clear, if Greg Gianforte becomes governor, expect progress on equal pay to come to a screeching halt.** He's donated thousands of dollars to the out-of-touch legislators that killed the Montana Paycheck Fairness Act last year and has to date declined to support it.

**This election matters. We can't stress that enough.** Closing the wage gap goes right to improving the family budget for many Montana households. Steve is partnering with businesses all over Montana to implement smart, data-based policies -- posting a salary range when advertising a position, not asking about previous compensation, promising not to retaliate against employees who request wage level information, creating an Equal Pay Hotline -- to give women the tools they need to be equally compensated.

**Greg Gianforte has shown over and over again that he just doesn't get it. Steve does.[Donate $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=5O00frvaHNDNdoSsn%2FIVZcusVmiGtNq9)**

Thanks,

Team Bullock

name: Hillary Clinton
subject: Peter, are you interested in being a fall fellow?
body:
| Peter --

I spent the summer of 1972 in San Antonio, Texas, walking block after block, knocking on door after door, helping register people to vote -- a lot of them for the first time.

Meeting all those prospective voters made an impact that has stayed with me to this day. **[I want you to have the same opportunity for that kind of experience. That’s why I’m reaching out to you personally, Peter, to see if you’d join our fellowship team this fall.**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjYxODE4MTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYxNDAwOTk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

As a fellow, you’ll be responsible for going out in your community and talking with voters about what’s at stake in this election. We need people like you to have those one-on-one interactions that are so crucial to winning campaigns -- and to empowering our fellow Americans to make their voices heard as part of our democracy. Our team will give you all the training you need to feel confident and make a real difference in this election.

This kind of grassroots effort propelled President Obama to the presidency (twice!) -- and it’s the same kind of effort that helped our team win more votes than any other candidate in this year’s primary, including Donald Trump. Trump may believe that he “alone” can fix our nation’s problems, but we know that we truly are stronger together -- and when we work side by side, we can help our country rise together.

We’d love for you to join us as we make history this fall, and as we fight for the values that have defined America for the last 240 years: love, inclusivity, and equality. **Add your name to learn more about becoming a fellow and making a big difference in this campaign:**
[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjYxODE4MTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYxNDAwOTk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
I hope you’ll join the team, Peter -- and maybe I’ll see you out on the trail! Thank you,

Hillary

With your help we can make it clear on Tuesday that Vermonters will not put up with false, negative attack ads and tactics, and that we stand with Phil Scott because of his integrity, leadership and ideas to improve our economy and make Vermont more affordable!

Team Scott

name: Jay Inslee
subject: FWD: A governor who does the right thing.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Now, more than ever, we must stand up for our values.

Dear Friend,

I've heard from so many Washingtonians this past week who are appalled at Donald Trump's recent attacks on the Gold Star Khan family.

Let me say this: Donald Trump is a coward. His attacks against the parents of a fallen soldier are vulgar and offensive. This is just one more instance of Islamophobia. Just like banning Muslims from coming to America, this hateful and offensive rhetoric demands our action.

Will you join me and Rita Zawaideh? Add your name today to speak out against Islamophobia and affirm our values of tolerance and inclusion.

LINK: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTMyNDEzMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=OTgwNjQ5MTc1S0&mt=1&rt=0

I am so proud to call Rita a friend -- her bold, compassionate leadership on behalf of refugees is inspiring. We are so very fortunate that she calls Washington home.

I truly believe that it’s never been more important to stand together for values that lift up our communities, welcome new Washingtonians, and ensure that love always trumps hate. That’s why I’m so proud to have you standing with me and Rita.

Our state -- and our country -- need to take a stand against Trump. Add your voice today.

LINK: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTMyNDEzMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=OTgwNjQ5MTc1S0&mt=1&rt=0

Very truly yours,

Jay Inslee

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Rita Zawaideh, JayInslee.com <info@jayinslee.com>
Date: Sat, Aug 6, 2016 at 9:30 AM
Subject: A governor who does the right thing

Dear Friend,

Here in Washington, we have a governor who does the right thing -- even during tough times. Case in point: Jay Inslee's decision to welcome refugees to our state wasn't just the right thing to do, it was deeply personal to me.

I had been organizing tours in the Middle East for years, so when the refugee crisis began in Syria, I heard from Washingtonians all over the state who wanted to help. It's in that moment of crisis that I decided to start an organization dedicated to helping the refugees in Syria. To me, helping seemed like the only option.

And Governor Jay Inslee agreed.

Now, more than ever, we need to fight back against the hatred and division in this country. I'm proud to support our Washington values of tolerance and inclusion, but those values cannot stand alone. We must all come together and affirm our support.

Will you join me today and add your name in support of these Washington values?

LINK: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTMyNDEzMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=OTgwNjQ5MTc1S0&mt=1&rt=0

As we know, too many leaders in our country and across the world decided to let hate and fear dictate their decision to close their doors to refugees. Not our governor. At a time when the easy political decision would have been to close our doors and our hearts, Jay Inslee took a stand on the side of justice. He chose to do the right thing by welcoming refugees to our state.

I'm so proud to call Jay Inslee my governor. Today, with the fear-mongering and hate we're seeing in our national political discourse -- Governor Inslee's compassionate, inclusive brand of progressive leadership is more important than ever.

Thanks to Jay Inslee, our state is more welcoming, more tolerant, and safer. But now, we're seeing threats to these values. Republicans like Donald Trump and Jay's opponent are spreading messages of fear and division.

Here's how we respond to this hate, together. Join me today to affirm your support for our Washington values of tolerance and inclusion -- and to stand on Jay's side.

LINK: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTMyNDEzMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=OTgwNjQ5MTc1S0&mt=1&rt=0

Jay's actions to support refugees say so much about his values. He's a leader who isn't afraid to do what's right, even if it isn't popular. This is the quality we want from our leaders.

I'm proud to support Jay.

Thank you for joining me,

Rita Zawaideh
Salaam Cultural Museum

SIGN ON: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTMyNDEzMwS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=OTgwNjQ5MTc1S0&mt=1&rt=0

| Friend,

**I’m honored to be running for Governor of Indiana, and I’m grateful for your continued support.**

Regardless of political party, economic status, or what city or town you call home, every Hoosier deserves to have someone in their corner working for them. We’ve come a long way as a state in the past twelve years by improving our infrastructure, creating high-paying jobs, and building our economy. I’m running to make sure we continue that success and continue moving Indiana forward.

**That’s why I need you now as a grassroots supporter of our campaign — we’re all in this together.**

By Wednesday we’ll be **90 days away from Election Day**. That means we’ve got a lot of ground to cover between now and then – so I need you to [**join Team Holcomb today as a Founding Member of our grassroots army > > >**](http://links.iterable.com/u/5218d2c8ebfe413aaa71f89a7ae36d01?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=25f5b4db99cc4a1aad7ed98fd5bfe969&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_cta&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=GFR&utm_content=20160808-honored-16266-direct)

Starting a campaign from scratch with only three months until Election Day is no easy task. We need to recruit volunteers, equip them with campaign material, launch TV and radio advertisements, and organize rallies and events all across the state.

**_So today, we’re kicking our campaign into high gear._ We set a goal to raise $10,000 before Wednesday to guarantee that we have the tools and resources necessary to launch our campaign the right way so we win in November.**

**Simply put, we don’t have much time between now and Election Day, so we need _every_ conservative Hoosier to stand up and join us right now.**

[**Help us kick off our campaign strong and become one of the first Founding Members of Team Holcomb by contributing $10 today!**](http://links.iterable.com/u/89a43929d6f2495cb3ccd06fdcff077e?_e=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&_m=25f5b4db99cc4a1aad7ed98fd5bfe969&amount=10&utm_source=Iterable&utm_medium=text_body_amount&utm_term=Fundraising_Deadline&utm_campaign=GFR&utm_content=20160808-honored-16266-direct) |
